title: Cross-Merchant Micro-Transaction Card Validation Sweep
narrative: Fraudster distributes tiny authorization tests across 8-15 low-scrutiny merchant accounts within a 45-90 minute window to validate stolen card data while evading single-merchant velocity controls. Transactions cluster at $0.50-$2.99 to multiple digital goods merchants (ProductCD='W'), often targeting MCC 5815 (digital media) and 5968 (subscription services). Each merchant sees only 1-2 transactions per card, but the cardholder experiences 10+ micro-charges in rapid succession. Pattern exploits merchant-siloed fraud detection systems. Successful validation precedes same-day or next-day high-value fraud at unrelated merchants. Email domains rotate across free providers (P_emaildomain in gmail.com, yahoo.com, outlook.com pool). Billing and shipping addresses often null or mismatched (dist1 > 5000km). Night-time transactions (txn_hour 1-5 AM merchant local time) account for 70%+ of attempts.
indicators: card1_txn_count_1hr between 8-15 with TransactionAmt all under $3.00
ProductCD exclusively 'W' (digital/virtual goods) across all test charges
P_emaildomain cycles through 3+ free providers within same session
dist1 exceeds 3000km or null in 80%+ of transactions
txn_hour clusters 01:00-05:00 in merchant timezone for 70%+ of attempts
card1_amt_zscore remains below 0.5 during testing phase, then spikes above 3.0 within 24 hours
summary: A single card generates 8-15 tiny purchases (under $3) across multiple different digital goods merchants within 1-2 hours, typically between 1-5 AM, with mismatched or missing address data and rotating free email providers.
reasoning: Criminals test stolen cards with micro-transactions that slip under individual merchant radar, then use validated cards for large fraudulent purchases within 24 hours at different stores.
indicator_explanations: Card is being tested with small-dollar charges to verify it's still active
Attacker is hitting multiple unrelated merchants to avoid single-merchant fraud blocks
All purchases are virtual goods that ship instantly with no physical address verification
Email address rotates between Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook to evade linking
Location data is absent or shows impossible geography across the testing run
Testing happens in the dead of night when fraud monitoring is lightest
Tiny test amounts suddenly jump to large fraudulent purchase after validation succeeds
